errorSessionExpired.message: Your session has expired. Please open the extension options and log in again.

errorGenericServer.message: The server returned an error. Please open the extension options and log in again.

extensionDescription.message: Temp mail: create disposable email addresses in one click and protect your real address from spam. Formerly TrashMail.com.

optionsNotifyExpired.message: Notify me when my account has expired

optionsSendURL.message: Send URL to Aionda Mail server

optionsSendURLTooltip.message: Sends the current URL of your browser to the Aionda Mail server when requesting a new disposable email address

optionsContact.message: Send bug reports and feature requests to $MAILTO$

welcomeTitle.message: Welcome to the Aionda Mail Add-on

registerIntro.message: Pick a username, set a password, enter your real email address - done. You will be signed in right away.

registerPasswordTooWeak.message: Password too weak: at least 8 characters; more length, mixed case, digits and special characters increase the strength.

registerUsernameRules.message: 3-30 characters: lowercase letters, digits, dots and hyphens only (not at the start/end, no doubles).

registerEmailInfo.message: Your disposable addresses will forward to this address. You will receive a confirmation email.

welcomeInfo.message: To create disposable email addresses, you will require a Aionda Mail account. With this account you will be able to use this add-on on multiple computers and browsers.

lostPasswordInfo.message: If your password has been lost, then a password reset will be necessary. Please enter your real email address which is associated with your username:

lostPasswordSuccess.message: $PBEGIN$Password reset link sent to '$EMAIL$'.$PEND$$PBEGIN$Please check your inbox for the password reset link.$PEND$

optionsInternalMailboxTooltip.message: Store emails in your Aionda Mail vault instead of forwarding them

info2FA.message: Enter the 6-digit code from your authenticator app.

error2FARequired.message: $STRONG$Two-Factor Authentication Required$STRONGEND$$BR$$BR$Your account has 2FA enabled. To use the browser extension, you need to create a Personal Access Token:$BR$$BR$1. Click the button below to open Aionda Mail$BR$2. Go to $STRONG$Account → Personal Access Tokens$STRONGEND$$BR$3. Create a new token and copy it$BR$4. Your $STRONG$username stays the same$STRONGEND$, only paste the token as your "Password"$BR$$BR$$BUTTON$

button.content: <button type="button" id="btn-open-access-tokens" class="button">Open Personal Access Tokens</button>

guardianKeyNeedsRenewal.message: Signature valid, but key needs renewal

guardianSecurityErrorMissing.message: Server response is not signed. Possible MITM attack!

guardianSecurityErrorMismatch.message: Signature verification failed. Possible MITM attack!

guardianInfoText.message: This feature protects you against Man-in-the-Middle (MITM) attacks. Corporate proxies like ZScaler or malicious networks could intercept your connection and read or modify your data. Aionda Mail signs every response with a cryptographic signature that this extension verifies. If someone tampers with the data, the signature won't match and you'll be warned immediately.

guardianFeature1.message: Ed25519 cryptographic signatures verify every server response

guardianFeature2.message: Detects tampering or header stripping by proxies (ZScaler, Fortinet, etc.)

guardianFeature3.message: Real-time verification with instant warnings if data is modified

guardianTlsProxyWarning.message: This could be a corporate proxy (ZScaler, Netskope, etc.) or a malicious attacker.

guardianTlsUnreachable.message: Could not verify certificate - server unreachable

guardianMissingSignatureReason1.message: A Man-in-the-Middle attack stripping security headers

guardianEnableInOptions.message: Can be enabled in the settings

optionsGuardianTooltip.message: Warns when server responses have been tampered with. Antivirus software or corporate proxies that scan HTTPS traffic can trigger false alarms.

guardianNotificationMitm.message: Your connection may be intercepted! Click for details.

guardianNotificationMissing.message: Response signature missing! Your connection may be compromised.

guardianChromeNoTls.message: TLS certificate verification not available in Chrome
